notes.content: UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals. Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.
notes.contentHtml:
UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals.


Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.

notes.content: The Assistive Technology minor is designed for students who are interested in helping people with movement disabilities. The emphasis is on designing exoskeletons and robots built on two core cross-disciplinary areas: Mechatronics and Functional Anatomy. This minor cannot be combined with the Assistive Technology: Motor concentration of the former bioengineering major or the Robotics Engineering B.S. major. Visit the Baskin Engineering Undergraduate Experience website to learn more about this minor. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Assistive Technology Minor Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
The Assistive Technology minor is designed for students who are interested in helping people with movement disabilities. The emphasis is on designing exoskeletons and robots built on two core cross-disciplinary areas: Mechatronics and Functional Anatomy. This minor cannot be combined with the Assistive Technology: Motor concentration of the former bioengineering major or the Robotics Engineering B.S. major.


Visit the Baskin Engineering Undergraduate Experience website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Assistive Technology Minor Program Statement
